channel.title: Morning Money
channel.description: Delivered daily by 8 a.m., Morning Money examines the latest news in finance, politics and policy — and on Fridays, Morning Money: Capital Risk highlights the downstream effects on markets.

item.title: Higher for longer for oil and rates
item.description: Wall Street is now pricing in a 30 percent likelihood that rates will be higher after the Fed’s final meeting of 2026.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: ‘Nobody knows’
item.description: The last year hasn’t been an easy one for Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: The Fed’s flying blind
item.description: The world’s central banks are likely to strike a more wary tone on inflation in a flurry of meetings this week.
item.dc:creator: By Victoria Guida

item.title: There’s always a housing angle
item.description: Concerns about future inflation have pushed up yields on government debt that influence the pricing of home loans.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: SBF’s pardon push falls flat on the Hill
item.dc:creator: By Jasper Goodman

item.title: Livin’ la Vida Boca
item.description: Wall Street has entered its prediction market era.
item.dc:creator: By Declan Harty

item.title: Smoke on the water
item.description: When things sound too good to be true, they often are.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: Political panic at the disco
item.description: It’s starting to feel like 1979.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: The Fed’s oil shock
item.description: The war in Iran is scrambling the economic outlook for the Federal Reserve.
item.dc:creator: By Victoria Guida and Ben Lefebvre
item.dc:contributor: By Victoria Guida and Ben Lefebvre

item.title: Why Trump’s iron fist can’t solve crypto’s war with Wall Street
item.description: Trump’s move to effectively align himself with crypto’s position is unlikely to force banks to cave.
item.dc:creator: By Jasper Goodman

item.title: Trump brings Warren and Scott together — against Wall Street
item.description: Congress is closing in on its first housing affordability bill in a decade, and it's offering a revealing lesson in how bipartisanship works in the Trump era.
item.dc:creator: By Katherine Hapgood

item.title: Will Treasury markets get yippy?
item.description: If there's one thing President Donald Trump responds to, it's the Treasury markets.
item.dc:creator: By Aiden Reiter

item.title: Of missiles and markets
item.description: Commodity investors sent oil prices up more than 10 percent in early trading as they eyed the possibility of extensive disruptions to energy supply.
item.dc:creator: By Declan Harty and Victoria Guida
item.dc:contributor: By Declan Harty and Victoria Guida

item.title: Washington is betting big on AI IPOs. What happens if they crack?
item.description: The artificial intelligence boom is coming to Wall Street — and Washington has a stake in the outcome.
item.dc:creator: By Yasmin Khorram

item.title: The retirement savings idea tucked in Trump’s speech
item.description: President Donald Trump rolled out one new talking point that could expand the ability of more American workers to save for retirement.
item.dc:creator: By Victoria Guida

item.title: The CFPB’s big day in court may not be all it’s cracked up to be
item.description: This afternoon, the D.C. Circuit Court of Appeals will hear a case on one of the administration’s more controversial moves.
item.dc:creator: By Aiden Reiter

item.title: More stability, less certainty
item.description: Trump can still bark, but without IEEPA tariffs, any bite that follows won’t be as immediate or vicious.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: Next up for regulators: liquidity
item.description: The next banking regulatory battle is starting to take shape, this time over rules governing how much cash banks need to hold to weather a crisis.
item.dc:creator: By Aiden Reiter

item.title: Trump wants foreign investment. Then the taxman wanted a cut.
item.description: The president’s tax regulator has proposed new rules that would strip away advantages that exempt sovereign wealth funds and other foreign government-controlled entities from paying taxes on some of their U.S. investments.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: Kevin Warsh’s monetary dominance monster
item.description: If Warsh has big plans for how to address the Fed’s balance sheet, he may have to temper his expectations.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: A new valentine for crypto
item.description: The shifting focus is the latest reminder of the dramatic shift that the SEC has taken toward crypto under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: By Declan Harty and Sam Sutton
item.dc:contributor: By Declan Harty and Sam Sutton

item.title: Trust us: The labor market’s fine
item.description: In a midterm election that will be defined by cost-of-living issues, the relative strength of a “low-hire, low-fire” economy won’t be a great sales pitch.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton and Declan Harty
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Sutton and Declan Harty

item.title: Another day, another dollar hangover
item.description: The greenback can’t catch a break.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: The tariff man taketh
item.description: New research from the nonpartisan Tax Foundation found that the president’s trade policies cost U.S. households about $1,000 on average last year.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: Skinny accounts on the horizon
item.description: The battle between crypto upstarts and banking stalwarts is moving to the Federal Reserve.
item.dc:creator: By Aiden Reiter

item.title: Meet Kevin Warsh
item.description: Warsh’s evolution from crisis-era inflation hawk to Trump ally has been headscratching for some Fed watchers, and some are “wondering which version of Warsh will ultimately govern the central bank.”
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: Wall Street draws a line on AI
item.description: AI has become an engine of the S&P 500, a central pillar of the U.S. economy and a core part of President Donald Trump’s economic agenda.
item.dc:creator: By Yasmin Khorram

item.title: Why Warsh might not give Trump the economy he wants
item.description: President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Federal Reserve would inherit a healthy economy that most Americans believe is about to skid.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: Another crypto divide simmers on Capitol Hill
item.description: A fracture is emerging within the cryptocurrency industry over how to decipher part of a sweeping digital assets bill.
item.dc:creator: By Declan Harty

item.title: Wall Street has already moved on from Jerome Powell
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has simultaneously become one of the most and least interesting people in the news.
item.dc:creator: By Victoria Guida and Sam Sutton
item.dc:contributor: By Victoria Guida and Sam Sutton
